summary: Frontend-only theme switching test on deployed Expo web preview (mobile viewport 390x844, demo login). The 3 segmented pills (setting-theme-light/dark/system) are present and the Settings screen itself themes correctly: bg toggles white→black on Dark click, dark pill gains primary border, AsyncStorage persists across page.reload(), and System mode follows page.emulate_media({colorScheme}) without re-tap. HOWEVER 3 critical regressions block the feature from being production-ready: (1) RED-SCREEN CRASH in home.tsx MenuItem (line 1139) — `styles is not defined` — fires the moment the user opens the kebab menu (header-menu-button). MenuItem is declared OUTSIDE the Home component so it cannot capture the local `styles` produced by `useMemo(() => mkStyles(Colors), [Colors])`. This breaks the canonical Home → Settings navigation path; the only way to reach Settings is direct URL. (2) The Light/Dark/System refactor missed MANY hardcoded `'#FFF'`/`'#FFFFFF'` backgrounds in `mkStyles()`-wrapped StyleSheets — so on /home in dark mode the Capital card AND the entire ACTIONS bottom panel are stark white on a black background (verified visually). Same regression in login.tsx, preset.tsx, and src/components/BottomSheet.tsx (affects every BottomSheet modal app-wide: max-loss, single-position, positions-menu). (3) Default mode is `system`, not `light` — contradicting the spec line `Light is the initial active pill`. On first launch with no persisted value, ThemeProvider line 41 hydrates `'system'` so the System pill (not Light) shows the primary border.

ui_bugs.issue: RED SCREEN: `ReferenceError: styles is not defined` at home.tsx:1139 / call site home.tsx:1150. MenuItem is a module-scope function and references `styles` (line 1150) + `Colors.danger` (line 1151) — both are now scoped inside Home() via mkStyles(Colors). Crashes when user taps header-menu-button (kebab). Fix: move MenuItem inside Home() so it closes over `styles`, OR pass styles/Colors as props.

Hardcoded `backgroundColor: '#FFF'` / `'#FFFFFF'` in: card (L1201, Capital card) → stays white in dark mode; sheetSafeBg (L1299) + sheetSurface (L1302) → ACTIONS bottom panel stays white in dark mode; menuPanel (L1402) → kebab menu stays white; bottomSheet (L1418) → max-loss / single-pos / positions-menu sheets stay white. Replace with Colors.surface / Colors.surfaceElevated / Colors.bg. Note: expiryPopover (L1466) + expiryCaret (L1481) are intentional per spec — leave as-is.

Two hardcoded `backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF'` (lines 108, 110) — the modal sheet bg never themes to dark. Every consumer (BottomSheet, ConfirmSheet, OrderConfirmSheet, UnderlyingSearchSheet, max-loss-sheet, single-position-sheet, positions-menu-sheet) renders white-on-white text in dark mode. Replace with `Colors.surface`.
ui_bugs.testID: max-loss-sheet, single-position-sheet, positions-menu-sheet, underlying-search-sheet, order-confirm-sheet, confirm-logout, confirm-exit

ui_bugs.issue: Hardcoded `backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF'`/'#FFF' at lines 578, 586, 678 — login screen card surfaces stay white in dark mode. Replace with theme palette.
Hardcoded `backgroundColor: '#FFF'` at line 441 — preset detail card stays white in dark mode.

Spec says `Light is the initial active pill`. Actual initial pill (no AsyncStorage value yet) is SYSTEM because ThemeProvider.tsx:41 hydrates the default to 'system'. On a fresh install in a light-OS test environment the visible bg happens to be white, but the active pill border is on SYSTEM, not Light. Either change the default to 'light' to match the spec, or update the spec/test expectation.

integration_issues.issue: Crashes immediately on kebab tap (RED SCREEN, `styles is not defined`). User must type /settings into the URL to access the theme switcher. Same crash blocks Refresh / Order History / Disconnect menu items.

issues: Capital card renders stark white (#FFF) on the black (#000) safe-area — extreme contrast, looks broken (verified via screenshot s3_home_dark.png).
ACTIONS bottom sheet renders stark white on black with light icons that are now invisible against the still-white surface — the entire bottom 40% of the screen is white.
Header chips and positions area DO theme correctly because they use Colors.* — only the hardcoded #FFF surfaces are stuck.

design_issues.screen: Any BottomSheet modal in Dark mode
issues: Modal sheet surface (#FFFFFF) clashes with dark-themed parent — text inside (which uses Colors.text = #F3F4F6 in dark) becomes near-invisible white-on-white.

react_native_issues.issue: Warning fired during render: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Multiple StyleSheet.create blocks across the app (e.g. home.tsx sheetSurface L1310-1313, menuPanel L1405-1407, toast L1524-1527, expiryPopover L1470-1473) still use shadowColor/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius/shadowOffset. Reference: home.tsx:1310

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Move MenuItem from module scope (home.tsx:1138) INSIDE the Home() component so it captures the local `styles`/`Colors` from the mkStyles factory. Alternatively pass `styles`, `Colors` as props. Reproduction: login → tap header-menu-button (kebab ⋮) → red screen 'styles is not defined'.

FIX CRITICAL: Replace every hardcoded '#FFF'/'#FFFFFF' inside `mkStyles(Colors)` blocks with the appropriate `Colors.*` token. Specific sites: home.tsx L1201 (card → Colors.surface), L1299 sheetSafeBg + L1302 sheetSurface (→ Colors.surface or Colors.bg), L1402 menuPanel (→ Colors.surfaceElevated), L1418 bottomSheet (→ Colors.surface). DO NOT change L1466 expiryPopover or L1481 expiryCaret (spec says these stay white intentionally). Same fix needed in login.tsx (L578, L586, L678), preset.tsx (L441), and src/components/BottomSheet.tsx (L108, L110).

DECIDE: Either change ThemeProvider default from 'system' to 'light' (matches the task spec '“Light is the initial active pill”') OR update the spec — currently the System pill is active on first launch.

FIX MEDIUM: Replace deprecated `shadow*` style props with `boxShadow` to silence the runtime warning (RN Web 0.21+). Affected: home.tsx multiple places, plus any other mkStyles using shadowColor/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius/shadowOffset.
critical_code_review_comments: ThemeProvider.tsx + theme.ts: design is sound — ColorPalette type, LightColors/DarkColors export, useTheme() hook with mkStyles(Colors) factory pattern is the right approach. Hydration loaded-flag (line 35-66) correctly avoids the light-to-dark flash. setMode persists to AsyncStorage key 'theme_mode' as documented. Issue is purely in consumer screens that forgot to migrate hardcoded hexes.
_layout.tsx ThemedShell (line 25-37): correctly tints SafeAreaProvider and Stack contentStyle with Colors.bg. Works as advertised — verified `body { background: rgb(0,0,0) }` in dark mode.

settings.tsx Appearance section (line 130-153): the 3-pill segmented selector is correctly implemented. testIDs match spec. Active pill styling (themeOptionActive: borderColor Colors.primary, surfaceElevated bg) works. Pill border is the only signal of active state — emoji + label color are subtle but readable. This screen itself is the only one that fully themes correctly in dark mode.

home.tsx MenuItem (line 1138-1154): structural bug. Must be inside Home() or accept styles/Colors as props. Currently silently shadows the module-level `Colors` import (LightColors) for the danger color even after fix — making the disconnect item always use light-red.

Pattern is repeated in multiple screens: the mkStyles refactor only swept named tokens (Colors.bg, Colors.text, etc.) but missed the dozen+ literal '#FFF' / '#FFFFFF' surfaces. Recommend a project-wide grep for `backgroundColor: "#` inside files that import useTheme — every match in a mkStyles factory is suspect.

success_rate.frontend: 55% — theme switching mechanics on /settings work (pills, palette swap, persistence, system-follow all verified) but dark mode is visually broken on /home + every BottomSheet, and the canonical kebab navigation path crashes.

seed_data_creation: None. Used POST /api/auth/login {api_key:demo, api_secret:demo} → demo token.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login via demo (api_key=demo, api_secret=demo). On /home the kebab (header-menu-button) currently CRASHES with red screen — navigate to /settings via direct URL until home.tsx MenuItem is fixed. Theme switching itself is wired correctly: setting-theme-light/dark/system pills work, AsyncStorage 'theme_mode' persists, and page.emulate_media({colorScheme}) drives the System mode. After main agent fixes (1) MenuItem scope, (2) hardcoded #FFF surfaces in home/login/preset/BottomSheet, retest: tap Dark → go to /home → confirm capital card + ACTIONS panel both have black/dark surfaces; open expiry popover + underlying-search modal in dark → confirm expiry pills stay white-with-dark-text per spec and underlying-search sheet themes correctly.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of the dark-mode regression on /home: the mkStyles(Colors) refactor was incomplete. Multiple StyleSheet entries kept LITERAL hex backgrounds (`backgroundColor: '#FFF'`) instead of being parametrised on Colors. Because mkStyles produces a fresh StyleSheet for each palette, these literal hexes silently override the palette — the surfaces visually break the moment the parent View flips to a dark Colors.bg. Root cause of the kebab crash: the local-state refactor (`styles = useMemo(...)`) moved `styles` from module to component scope, but MenuItem — declared at module scope at line 1138 — was not updated. JS closure rules: MenuItem cannot see `styles` (it's not in any enclosing scope), so any render of MenuItem throws `ReferenceError: styles is not defined`. The error is caught by the LogBox error boundary which is why the UI doesn't fully die — but the kebab menu never renders. Reproduction: deployed env → demo login → tap ⋮ kebab in home header → red screen overlay.
